questions.question: What does Euclid’s Division Algorithm state?
questions.correct_answer: For any positive integers a and b, a = bq + r, where 0 ≤ r < b
questions.explanation: It expresses a number as a multiple of another plus a remainder.

questions.question: What is the main use of Euclid’s Division Algorithm?
questions.correct_answer: To find the HCF of two positive integers
questions.explanation: Repeated division helps determine the highest common factor.

questions.question: State the Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic.
questions.correct_answer: Every composite number can be expressed as a unique product of primes
questions.explanation: Uniqueness is apart from the order of prime factors.

questions.question: What is meant by prime factorisation?
questions.correct_answer: Expressing a number as a product of prime numbers
questions.explanation: It uses only prime numbers as factors.

questions.question: How is HCF found using prime factorisation?
questions.correct_answer: By multiplying the smallest powers of common prime factors
questions.explanation: Only common primes with least powers are considered.

questions.question: How is LCM found using prime factorisation?
questions.correct_answer: By multiplying the greatest powers of all prime factors
questions.explanation: All primes involved are taken with highest powers.

questions.question: What is an irrational number?
questions.correct_answer: A number that cannot be written in the form p/q
questions.explanation: Its decimal expansion is non-terminating and non-repeating.

questions.question: Which method is used to prove √2 is irrational?
questions.explanation: Assuming √2 is rational leads to a contradiction.

questions.question: What happens when a rational number is added to an irrational number?
questions.explanation: Sum or difference of rational and irrational is irrational.

questions.question: Is the product of a non-zero rational and an irrational number rational?

notes.title: Euclid’s Division Algorithm
notes.content: It is used to express a number in terms of another with a remainder and is helpful in finding HCF.

notes.title: Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic
notes.content: Prime factorisation of a composite number is always unique except for the order of primes.

notes.title: HCF and LCM Relation
notes.content: For two numbers a and b, HCF × LCM = a × b.

notes.title: Irrational Numbers
notes.content: Irrational numbers cannot be represented as fractions and have non-repeating decimals.

notes.title: Proof by Contradiction
notes.content: A method where assuming the opposite leads to a logical contradiction.

facts.fact: Every composite number has at least one prime factorisation.
HCF × LCM = product of numbers holds true only for two numbers.
Sum or difference of rational and irrational numbers is always irrational.
